sprint.goal: Harden enforcement runtime — fix all P0/P1 governance bugs before dogfood run
sprint.focus: P0/P1 COMPLETE: all governance bugs closed or in green PR. PR #89 (test coverage + dead code) CI green, awaiting human merge.

p1.title: bug: bounded-execution policy timeout (300s) is silently overridden to 60s in shell execution
bug: classifyShellRisk prefix matching too broad — false read-only classification
test: zero test coverage across all packages

p2.title: bug: dead code in flattenParams() overwrites result before using it
bug: scheduler.go silently ignores os.WriteFile error
Dogfood: run ShellForge swarm on jared box via RunPod GPU
p2.notes: Blocked on setup.sh: isServer=true skips Goose, remote Ollama (OLLAMA_HOST) not supported

p2.title: bug: filepath.Glob with ** in cmdScan never matches any Go files
bug: run() helper in main.go silently ignores command errors
bug: kernel version comparison in setup.sh is lexicographic, not numeric
bug: InferenceQueue is not priority-aware despite being documented as such
bug: run-qa-agent.sh and run-report-agent.sh don't build binary if missing
bug: agent RunResult.Success heuristic is incorrect
bug: listFiles() returns paths relative to cwd, not the listed directory

p3.title: feat: OpenClaw as governed execution runtime in ShellForge

[research] RTK integration — 70-90% token savings for agent runs
